title: The Big Picture: Iron ore improving
section_path: The Big Picture: Iron ore improving
section_path_text: The Big Picture: Iron ore improving
summary:
Main
By Mark Nugent

Shipments growing in May
As weather in Brazil starts to subside and the end of the financial year for several miners approaches, we look at the iron ore market and what may be in store going forward. Demand outside of China strong
Global iron ore shipments totalled 131.5m tonnes in April, coming in flat YoY. However, vessel demand from iron ore increased by 6% YoY in April on the back of stronger liftings in the Capesize sector, while demand from the smaller segments all declined during the same period.

children.title: Shipments growing in May
children.summary: As weather in Brazil starts to subside and the end of the financial year for several miners approaches, we look at the iron ore market and what may be in store going forward.

children.title: Demand outside of China strong
children.summary: Global iron ore shipments totalled 131.5m tonnes in April, coming in flat YoY. However, vessel demand from iron ore increased by 6% YoY in April on the back of stronger liftings in the Capesize sector, while demand from the smaller segments all declined during the same period.

children.title: Brazil gradually returns
children.summary: Out of Brazil, iron ore liftings have improved in recent weeks fuelling more positive sentiment in the Capesize market. Shipments totalled 25.8m tonnes in April, the highest monthly total so far this year.

children.title: Relaxed restrictions lift Australian liftings
children.summary: In Australia, production constraints that miners have faced, namely labour shortages due to travel restrictions, have started to subside as the country moves out of lockdowns. As expected, this has vastly improved the overall efficiency of miners' operations in the country.

children.title: India stronger in April
children.summary: Indian iron ore exports totalled 4.4m tonnes in April, the highest since March 2021. While steel mills continue to face higher costs because of elevated energy and coking coal prices, a lower grade iron ore may provide some relief to producers' bottom line.

children.title: Longer term impact of Ukraine
children.summary: While no shipments have occurred since late February, the majority of Ukrainian iron ore typically shipped to the Far East, namely China and Japan. In finding replacement volumes, these countries have naturally looked to Australia and India given their proximity.
